title: (No AI) China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
author: Robert Hart
content:
![SHANGHAI, CHINA - JULY 20 2026: People visit the booth of Kimi, an LLM developed by the Chinese startup Moonshot, during the World AI Conference in Shanghai, China, Monday, July 20, 2026. (Photo credit should read LONG WEI/ Feature China/Future Publishing via Getty Images)](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/gettyimages-2286280034.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence.

The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which [unveiled Kimi K3](https://x.com/Kimi_Moonshot/status/2077830229968683203?s=20) on Friday. Moonshot [claims](https://www.kimi.com/blog/kimi-k3) its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/967781/chinese-ai-models-open-source-moonshot-kimi-k3-alibaba-qwen)
contentSnippet:
China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming central to national security, economic power, and geopolitical influence. 
The opening salvo came from Beijing-based Moonshot AI, one of China's leading AI model developers, which unveiled Kimi K3 on Friday. Moonshot claims its own testing ranks it consistently above nearly every US system, trailing only OpenAI …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is increasingly tight, just as the technology is becoming […]

title: (No AI) I hate that I don’t hate this song made with Suno
author: Terrence O’Brien
content:
![Artist 1010Benja in 2025 standing in a road wearing all black during golden hour.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Screenshot-2026-07-19-at-1.30.30-PM.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

1010Benja won’t apologize for using AI. | Image: 1010Benja / Instagram

I would never go so far as to say there's no place for AI in music (I'm a fan of Holly Herndon, after all). But I generally find music made with generative AI to be [offensively boring](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/965581/suno-ai-music-vergecast), especially the outputs of [Suno](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/786349/suno-ai-music-generator-v5-review). So I'm having a bit of a tough time processing the fact that I actually quite enjoy 1010Benja's "[Semiramis' Dream](https://youtu.be/XwKDmTxAqck?list=OLAK5uy_lYaoXcrvI8Tbc2saAjhHQQgQLebT-KVUw)."

Benja has been unapologetic about his use of generative AI on his latest EP, [_Time Has Nothing To Do With What You Choose…_](https://soundcloud.com/1010benja/sets/1010-time-has-nothing-to-do) The other three tracks can't quite hold a candle to what you find on 2024's [_Ten Total_](https://soundcloud.com/1010benja/sets/ten-total)_._ But the opener "Semiramis' Dream" is infectious. It explodes out of the speakers with a jungle beat and Be …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/967678/1010benja-semiramis-dream-suno-ai-music)

title: (No AI) A scorecard for the AI age

title: (No AI) How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
